subject: set max concurrent agens to 5
instructions:
context: So to answer your question: it uses your
  subscription (or API key) — whatever your local
  claude CLI is authenticated with. Each dispatched
  ticket spawns a full claude CLI session, which
  counts as normal Claude Code usage against your
  account.

  The max concurrency is set to 2 agents at a time
  (MAX_CONCURRENT = 2 in server/dispatcher.ts), which
   helps avoid burning through your rate limits too
  fast.
  ⎿  Stop says: Hookify import error: No module named
      'hookify'                               
                                                    
✻ Sautéed for 48s                                   
                              
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
❯ nah, put it up to 5 concurrent. i have a max pro   
  subscription. ra
lastOutput:
★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────
- Changed a single constant that gates concurrency across the entire dispatcher system
- The change is minimal (2→5) but impactful: allows 2.5× more parallel agent sessions
- With rate limits on a max pro subscription, 5 concurrent agents is a sweet spot between throughput and rate limit safety
─────────────────────────────────────────────────

Done! I've increased the `MAX_CONCURRENT` constant from 2 to 5 in `server/dispatcher.ts:6`. This will allow up to 5 agents to run in parallel, making better use of your max pro subscription's higher rate limits.

The change has been committed, pushed, and a pull request has been created.

https://github.com/jess-bergs/agent-kanban/pull/7
